'''Eorl Crabtree''' (born 2 October 1982) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 2000s and 2010s. He played at international level for [[England rugby league team|England]], and at club level in the [[Super League]] for the [[Huddersfield Giants]], where he spent his entire club career, primarily as a {{rlp|pr}}, but also as a {{rlp|ce|sr}} or {{rlp|lf}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

He is managing director of the [[Midlands Hurricanes]].

==Early years==
Born in [[Cleethorpes]], Lincolnshire, Crabtree has lived in [[Meltham]] since he was three years old and attended [[Honley High School]]. He is a distant nephew of legendary professional wrestler [[Shirley Crabtree|Shirley "Big Daddy" Crabtree]],<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/42097084|title = Will World Cup joy finally come for Sam Burgess?|work = BBC Sport}}</ref> himself a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played for [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford Northern]], although he never made an appearance for the first team.
[[File:Eorl Crabtree.jpg|thumb|left|75px|Crabtree while playing for Huddersfield in 2010]]

==Playing career==
===Club career===
Crabtree signed for Huddersfield on his 17th birthday and he made his début for the club in 2001. The following season he scored 25 tries in 37 league and cup appearances whilst playing at {{rlp|ce}} during Huddersfield's [[RFL Championship|National League]] promotion season. His breakthrough year in Super League was 2004 where, under coach [[Jon Sharp]], he established himself as a regular in Huddersfield's first team and developed a reputation as an imposing figure with his mix of size and athleticism. Crabtree played for Huddersfield in the [[2006 Challenge Cup]] Final as a {{rlp|sr}} against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] but the Giants lost 12–42. Crabtree had a superb 2009 campaign at [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]]. Having helped them to a third-place finish in the [[Super League]], and to [[Wembley Stadium|Wembley]] for the [[Challenge Cup]] final, Crabtree also impressed national coach [[Tony Smith (rugby league b. 1967)|Tony Smith]] who selected Eorl in the [[England national rugby league team|England]] squad for the [[2009 Four Nations]]. At the end of the year, Crabtree signed a new long-term contract until the end of the 2015 season.

Crabtree was an integral part of the Huddersfield team that finished top of [[Super League XVIII]], and was one of four Huddersfield players selected in the [[Super League Dream Team]] that year. In April 2014, Crabtree made his 350th appearance for the Giants in a 33–14 win over Warrington in [[Super League XIX]]. With this appearance he is currently the 14th most capped Huddersfield club player ever.<ref>{{cite news 
| title = Huddersfield Giants prop Eorl Crabtree reaches club 350 appearances
| publisher = The Huddersfield Daily Examiner
| date = 2 April 2014
| url = https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/news/huddersfield-giants-prop-eorl-crabtree-6901907
| access-date = 20 July 2022}}</ref> In 2015, he signed an extension to his contract taking him until the end of the 2017 season.<ref>[https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/32495543 Eorl Crabtree: Huddersfield Giants prop signs new deal]</ref> On 3 September 2015, Crabtree made history and became the 10th most capped club player of the Huddersfield Giants. He also scored a try on his 400th appearance for the club.<ref>{{cite news 
| title = Huddersfield beat Castleford to close on Super League semi-finals
| publisher = theguardian
| date = 3 September 2015
| url = https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/sep/03/huddersfield-giants-castleford-tigers-super-league-match-report
| access-date = 5 September 2015}}</ref>

Crabtree announced his retirement as a player on 7 November 2016 to become an ambassador for Huddersfield.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.giantsrl.com/news/article/5749/crabtree-calls-time-on-stellar|title=Crabtree calls time on stellar playing career|publisher=Huddersfield Giants|date=7 November 2016|access-date=7 November 2016}}</ref> He also made regular appearances as a commentator and pundit for [[BBC Sport]]'s rugby league coverage. In January 2024, Crabtree became the managing director of League 1 club the [[Midlands Hurricanes]] thus ending his long association with Huddersfield.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/eorl-crabtree-finds-new-role-following-huddersfield-giants-exit/|title=Eorl Crabtree finds new role following Huddersfield Giants exit|website=www.totalrl.com}}</ref>

=== International career ===
Crabtree played 13 times at international level for [[England national rugby league team|England]]. He featured in the [[2009 Four Nations|2009]] and [[2010 Rugby League Four Nations|2010]] Four Nations tournaments, but missed out on selection in 2011.
